scenario: First-time setup at project level with verification test
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) ask if guardrails apply to project or global, (2) install block-dangerous-git.sh to .claude/hooks/, (3) add PreToolUse hook entry to .claude/settings.json, (4) run verification test with 'git reset --hard' input, (5) receive exit code 2 and blocking message, (6) offer optional customization of blocked patterns
notes: Critical: verification test must pass before declaring DONE. Exit code 2 = actual block. Settings.json parse must succeed.

scenario: User wants to customize blocked patterns
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) install hook to ~/.claude/hooks/, (2) during Step 5 customization, ask user which patterns to add/remove, (3) edit BLOCKED_PATTERNS array in script, (4) re-run verification test with new patterns, (5) confirm updated guardrails active
notes: Optional step but MUST ask. Silence ≠ agreement to defaults.

scenario: Verify hook blocks destructive commands and allows safe ones
expected_behavior: Blocked commands (push, reset --hard, clean -fd) should receive exit code 2 with ⛔ message. Allowed commands (status, log) should exit code 0. Test should verify both blocking AND allowing work correctly.
notes: Test that hook is selective, not a blanket blocker. Safe commands must pass through.
